close_reason: Single-file crate at 2500 lines is manageable; splitting into modules adds complexity for no readability gain
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs is 2417 lines because the two parallel JSON scanners live in the same file as the SecretValue, SecretError, SecretUri, traits, blocking adapter, registration, and tests. The maintenance comment at lib.rs:121-160 already explicitly explains the scanner contract, which is a strong hint that this is its own subsystem.

Splitting into:
  src/lib.rs (re-exports)
  src/secret_value.rs
  src/json/scanner_char.rs   (json_parse_string et al.)
  src/json/scanner_byte.rs   (skip_value_b et al.)
  src/json/mod.rs            (json_extract_string_field, json_navigate)
  src/error.rs
  src/uri.rs
  src/traits.rs
  src/blocking.rs
  src/registration.rs
would let a contributor reading 'where is the JSON scanner' jump straight to it without scrolling past the URI parser, and would let the contract comment between the two scanners be enforced by separate-files visibility (each scanner can mark its internal helpers pub(crate)).

This is a P3 elegance fix — the code works fine as-is, but the file size invites scrolling-fatigue mistakes.
title: Two JSON scanner halves should live in their own modules
